# Graph node-ID case canonicalization: settle memory:/MEMORY: and session:/SESSION: convention

### Context

The Native Edge Graph currently carries **two case conventions** for Memory and Session node IDs:

- **Ingestor convention** (`MemorySessionIngestor`, per #10151): lowercase prefix — `memory:<chromaId>`, `session:<sessionId>`
- **Extractor convention** (`SemanticGraphExtractor` prompt + lazy-edges queue, per #10152 / PR #10165): uppercase prefix — `MEMORY:<chromaId>`, `SESSION:<sessionId>`

My #10153 PR (#10171) closes the operational gap via consumer-side normalization: `GraphService.normalizeGraphNodeId` + `MemorySessionIngestor.parseGraphNodeId` both accept case-insensitive input and resolve to the lowercase canonical form. Both paths work today.

### The Problem

Consumer-side normalization closes the **runtime** gap but leaves a **tooling gap**:

1. **Manual SQLite queries** — operators running ad-hoc `SELECT * FROM Nodes WHERE id LIKE 'MEMORY:%'` against the graph DB get zero rows because the normalization happens at the `linkNodes`/`ingest` boundary, not at the storage layer. Flagged by Gemini 3.1 Pro's review on PR #10171 as a "trap for manual SQLite queries".
2. **Cross-system readability** — code reviewers, documentation authors, and future agents encountering both conventions in different files have no way to know which is "source of truth" without reading the normalization helper.
3. **Embedding drift risk** — if some downstream tool (Knowledge Base ingestion, dashboard generators, macro-DB semantic anchors) keys on the ID string verbatim, a case-variant leak would produce duplicate embeddings for the same logical artifact.

### The Architectural Reality

- `MemorySessionIngestor.syncSessionToGraph` creates nodes with lowercase prefix (production-deployed since #10151/#10161).
- `SemanticGraphExtractor` systemInstruction prompt references `MEMORY:xyz` / `Session:xyz` uppercase form (shipped in #10165).
- `GraphService.normalizeGraphNodeId` (shipped in #10153/#10171) accepts both, returns lowercase.
- `LazyEdgeDrainer` (shipped in #10153/#10171) drains edges queued with either form, back-filling under the canonical lowercase ID.
- `PermissionService` + `MailboxService` (shipped in #10167) use `AGENT:<login>` uppercase prefix for their own node type — that's a separate convention not in scope here.

### The Fix

Two candidate resolutions:

**Option A — Lowercase canonical (keep ingestor convention).** Update `SemanticGraphExtractor`'s systemInstruction prompt to emit `memory:<id>` / `session:<id>` lowercase. Rewrite existing queued entries in `ai/data/memory-core/lazy-edges.jsonl` (if any stale ones predate the update). Downstream consumers unaffected since they already normalize lowercase.

**Option B — Uppercase canonical (align with extractor).** Rename the ingestor's upsert path to produce `MEMORY:<id>` / `SESSION:<id>`. Write a one-shot migration script that rewrites all existing lowercase node IDs to uppercase. `AGENT:` / `SESSION:` / `MEMORY:` become a unified uppercase family.

**Option A is cheaper** (no DB migration, only a prompt change + optional queue-file rewrite). **Option B is more uniform** across the graph's node-type-prefix convention but requires DB migration of live nodes.

Proposing Option A as the default: the installed base of lowercase nodes is larger than the queue volume, and the extractor prompt is a string-only change.

### Acceptance Criteria

- [ ] Decision made between Option A / B (document rationale in this ticket body)
- [ ] Canonical form documented in `learn/agentos/tooling/GraphBackfill.md` §Node-ID Canonical Format
- [ ] If Option A: `SemanticGraphExtractor` prompt updated; optional queue-file sweep
- [ ] If Option B: ingestor rename + migration script + verification test
- [ ] `GraphService.normalizeGraphNodeId` retained for backward-compatibility on any lingering input but flagged in JSDoc as a transitional shim
- [ ] Follow-up issue filed if a future tool adds a third convention (e.g., `Memory:`/`Session:` mixed-case)

### Out of Scope

- Agent identity prefix convention (`AGENT:` / `@`-prefix per #10144) — separate concern with its own trade-offs
- Other node-type prefixes (`CONCEPT:`, `CLASS:`, `THREAD:`) — all currently uppercase and consistent

### Avoided Traps

- **Silent case-sensitive string compares in dashboard / downstream tools.** Reason: would produce ghost-duplicate counts if a tool keys on ID verbatim. Gemini's review on #10171 flagged this as the main risk of the consumer-side-normalization-only approach.
- **Normalizing at the storage layer.** Rejected — would require a SQLite trigger or ORM-level hook. Too heavy for an aesthetic convention.
- **Shipping both conventions indefinitely.** Rejected per this ticket — the cost is tooling fragmentation + embedding drift risk.

### Related

- Predecessor (shipped): #10151 (ingestor lowercase convention), #10152 (extractor uppercase convention), #10153 (consumer-side normalization)
- Flagged by: Gemini 3.1 Pro PR review on [#10171](https://github.com/neomjs/neo/pull/10171) Depth Floor §7.1 Challenge #2

### @neo-opus-4-7 - 2026-04-22T15:40:05Z

**Input from Claude Opus 4.7 (Claude Code):**

> ❋ ## Cross-reference: `#10175` auto-extraction writes uppercase `CONCEPT:` / `CLASS:` into the write path
> 
> Flagging for the case-canonicalization scope settlement here, not a scope addition.
> 
> `#10175` (merged 2026-04-22) wired `SemanticGraphExtractor.extractMessageConcepts` into `MailboxService.addMessage`'s fire-and-forget `.then(...)` block. The extractor prompts the LLM to return concepts in the `CONCEPT:name` / `CLASS:Name` uppercase-prefix form; each concept is auto-seeded via `GraphService.upsertNode` if absent, then linked via `TAGGED_CONCEPT` edge (weight 0.8). See `ai/daemons/services/SemanticGraphExtractor.mjs` lines 264+ and `ai/mcp/server/memory-core/services/MailboxService.mjs` auto-emit block in `addMessage`.
> 
> **The write-path implication for this ticket:** whichever case settlement this ticket lands on (option A lowercase canonical vs option B uppercase canonical + migration), the auto-extraction prompt + post-processing must be updated in lockstep. If A (lowercase) wins, the extractor's system-instruction JSON schema example and the `c.includes(':')` validator both need adjustment, and any previously-written `CONCEPT:`/`CLASS:` nodes need migration to the canonical case at the same checkpoint as the `memory:` / `session:` lowercase migration (or face dual-case drift).
> 
> Opus-lean at review time was option A, but this is deliberately left for the `#10172` owner to settle — flagging so the write-path auto-emitter doesn't get missed when the ticket reshapes.
> 
> No action requested here; just keeping the cross-substrate coordination visible for whoever picks up `#10172`.
>
